# Ingredients

##### Backed by research

Curious how we made soda refreshing, delicious and actually good for you? It’s not magic or sorcery, but rather science. Ben, our co-founder and formulator, spent years searching for ingredients backed by research and historical evidence of working to fuel our microbiomes and improve our digestive health.

#### OLISmart: Our Functional Formula

Our functional formula, trademarked OLISmart, sets OLIPOP apart from other sodas. OLISmart contains a blend of botanicals, plant fibers, and prebiotics, each hand-picked for their distinct biome-supporting benefits. Nutritional information for our new shelf-stable multi-packs can be found here.

## Our OLISmart Ingredients

###### Cassava Root

Contributes prebiotic fiber

Cassava is a starchy tuber commonly cultivated and consumed across South America. This root contains resistant dextrin: a type of prebiotic fiber that can support gut health by providing food for the beneficial bacteria that live in your microbiome.

Learn More About Cassava Root

###### Chicory Root

A source of prebiotic fiber, inulin & flavinoids

A part of the dandelion family, chicory root is one of the most well-researched sources of inulin, a type of prebiotic fiber that supports microbiome health.

Learn More About Chicory Root

###### Jerusalem Artichoke

## What Is OLISMART? Get To Know the Ingredients in OLIPOP

Posted Feb 11, 2022 Updated Nov 20, 2025

If you've ever turned around a can of OLIPOP, you may have noticed an unfamiliar phrase on the ingredients panel, OLISMART, and thought to yourself, "What the heck is OLISMART?" Well, you've landed in the right place to find out! This unique blend of ingredients is what sets OLIPOP apart from other sodas, so let's dive into what OLISMART means, what's in it, and why we use it.

## What Is OLIPOP?

Gone are the days when you had to gulp down over 40 grams of sugar if you wanted a can of soda. With OLIPOP, you can enjoy the crisp, satisfying taste of the soda you grew up sipping, but with a fraction of the sugar, and the added benefits of microbiome and digestive health support.

It may sound like magic (soda that's actually good for you?!), but this sorcery is actually science. OLIPOP is the result of years of working alongside leading human gut microbiome researchers to find ingredients backed by science that fuel your microbiomes and support digestive health.

And that's where OLISMART comes in. OLISMART is our proprietary blend of unique botanicals, plant fibers, and prebiotics (the primo food source for beneficial bacteria), each hand-picked for their distinct biome-supporting benefits.

## What Is OLISMART?

Think of OLISMART as our (not-so) secre

## FAQ

Still Have Questions?

Below are some of the most common questions our customers have asked us! If you don't find what you're looking for, please send us an email at hello@drinkolipop.com.

Send an Email

- Ingredients
- Payment
- Product
- Subscription
- Shipping + Delivery
- Digestive Health
- General Questions

### General Questions

##### What exactly is OLIPOP?

OLIPOP is a new kind of soda that’s good for you and tastes good, too (not to mention super Instagrammable). Available in 18 delicious, classic soda flavors, OLIPOP combines functional ingredients, like prebiotics and fiber, to support digestive health—with 2-5g of sugar and 50 calories or less in each can. Head to our ingredients page for a closer look.

##### Why should I drink OLIPOP?

For starters, OLIPOP’s good. Like, really, really good. It combines the flavors you love with the digestive health support you need. Our fizzy, functional pop is packed with prebiotics and fiber to support your gut and give you the flavors you crave—all in one. Plus, it’s non-GMO, vegan, gluten-free, paleo, and keto-friendly.

##### Where can I find OLIPOP in stores?

OLIPOP is sold directly to consumers here on our website, plus we’re in almost 50,000 stores nationwide! To find OLIPOP at a store near you, use our handy store locator. In stores, you can find single-serve cans and select 4-packs of OLIPOP on refrigerated shelves, oft

ShareSaveWhere to buy
### Ingredients
Carbonated water, olismart (cassava root fiber, chicory root inulin, jerusalem artichoke inulin, nopal cactus\*, marshmallow root\*, calendula flower\*, kudzu root\*), quince juice concentrate, lemon juice, apple juice concentrate, cassava root syrup, key lime juice concentrate, stevia leaf\*, himalayan pink salt, natural key lime flavor.
\*extract
### Allergens
No allergen statement found. Please review the product label carefully and/or contact the manufacturer if you have a severe food allergy.

results.title: Ingredients – OLIPOP | Healthy Prebiotic Soda
results.text:
Ingredients – OLIPOP | Healthy Prebiotic Soda

# Ingredients

Ingredients

Backed by research

Curious how we made soda refreshing, delicious and actually good for you? It’s not magic or sorcery, but rather science. Ben, our co-founder and formulator, spent years searching for ingredients backed by research and historical evidence of working to fuel our microbiomes and improve our digestive health.

OLISmart: Our Functional Formula

Our functional formula, trademarked OLISmart, sets OLIPOP apart from other sodas. OLISmart contains a blend of botanicals, plant fibers, and prebiotics, each hand-picked for their distinct biome-supporting benefits.

Nutritional information for our new shelf-stable multi-packs can be found here.

Our OLISmart Ingredients

Cassava Root

Cassava Root

Contributes prebiotic fiber

Cassava is a starchy tuber commonly cultivated and consumed across South America. This root contains resistant dextrin: a type of prebiotic fiber that can support gut health by providing food for the beneficial bacteria that live in your microbiome.

Chicory Root

A source of prebiotic fiber, inulin & flavinoids

A part of the dandelion family, chicory root is one of the most well-researched sources of inulin, a type of prebiotic fiber that supports microbiome health.

Jerusalem Artichoke

Jerusalem Artichoke

Adds fiber, inulin, potassium, iron & antioxidants

This knobby tuber is not an artichoke but the root of a variety of sunflower. While it contains many beneficial micronutrients, Jerusalem artichoke’s best contribution is

Nopal Cactus

Used for its fiber, antioxidants & carotenoids

Commonly used in Mexican and Latin American cuisine, Nopal cactus is also utilized as an ingredient in beauty and medicinal products. It contains soothing soluble fibers pectin and mucilage along with antioxidants, minerals, and vitamins.

Calendula Flower

Rich in lutein, beta-carotene, and flavonoids

A golden flower that packs a healthy punch, calendula has a rich history and a sweet taste. Calendula petals contain antioxidants and have been used as an herbal remedy for a wide variety of ailments due to their healing effects.

Kudzu Root

Supplies a resistant starch that acts as a prebiotic

The root of a fast-growing vine, kudzu root has been used in Chinese herbal medicine for over 2,000 years as a remedy for certain ailments, such as stomach upset.

Marshmallow Root

A source of plant mucilage

Marshmallow root is an ingredient known for soothing a variety of ailments and was once used to make marshmallows. Many of marshmallow root’s alleviating abilities is due to its mucilage, a dense, glutinous material composed of protein and polysaccharides.

Acacia Fiber

Increases soluble dietary fiber content

Acacia fiber is one of OLIPOP’s prebiotic fibers. It is a soluble, non-viscous fiber from the dried, powdered sap of Acacia trees.

Guar Fiber

A source of soluble dietary fiber

Guar fiber is a fiber created from the seeds of a guar plant. It is high in

## Healthy Meets Delicious

## Our Foundational Ingredients

For over a decade, Ben and David have shared a vision of a healthier, tastier future. Their quest for a product that could undermine the old guard of soda behemoths led them to OLIPOP: a new kind of soda™ that’s delicious, refreshing, and actually good for you. It combines the classic soda flavor you love with functional ingredients that support your microbiome and digestive health.

## Our Scientific Advisory Board

We've assembled a global team of leading human gut microbiome research pioneers with diverse expertise spanning digestive health, prebiotic structures, model gut systems and gut microbiome-supportive nutritional strategies. We work with them to translate cutting edge research and findings into OLIPOP's product development.

#### Dr. Stephen Lindemann

Purdue University

Dr. Stephen Lindemann is an Assistant Professor in the Food Science and Nutrition Science Departments and Faculty Member of the Whistler Center for Carbohydrate Research at Purdue University. His laboratory focuses on how dietary fiber and prebiotic fine structure impacts gut microbiome diversity and function, genomic mechanisms of community polysaccharide fermentation and influence of carbohydrate structure on microbiome-mediated gut health. His research team is partnering with OLIPOP to investigat

## Understanding the OLIPOP Nutrition Label

Posted Feb 12, 2026 Updated Feb 13, 2026

When you're putting food or drink into your body, it's essential to know how that item stacks up nutrition-wise. And the best way to get that info is by reading the nutrition label. You can find this chart on the side panel or the back of the packaging. It gives a glimpse into exactly how nutritionally balanced that food or drink really is.

Every can of OLIPOP comes with a nutrition label, allowing you to know precisely what you’re getting when you crack open a can.

If you are a nutrition label-reading novice, don't fret. With a bit of know-how, you can easily navigate the words and values found on your can and make sense of it in a snap. Continue on to learn how to read and understand the OLIPOP nutrition label.

## OLIPOP Nutrition Facts

First, let's review the back nutrition label of an OLIPOP can. Here are the nutrition ranges you can expect to see on the label of our 12 fl oz can:

- Calories: 35–50
- Total Fat: 0g
- Sodium: 25–35mg
- Total Carbohydrate: 11–19g
- Dietary Fiber: 6–9g
- Total Sugars: 2–5g
- Added Sugars: 1–3g
- Protein: 0g
